text:
St Mary's Hospital 

Institute of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 16 January 2022 

Dr Ahmed  
Riverside Medical Centre  
42 Brook Street, London SE1 8QH  

Dear Dr Ahmed,  

Thomas Bennett, DOB: 12-04-1985, Hospital No: H938271 NHS No. 9054731126  
Flat 3, 19 Oakfield Road, London N4 2TX 

Medication: 

Levetiracetam 1000 mg morning / 1000 mg evening. He reports good adherence with minimal side effects, mainly mild fatigue that does not limit daily activities. No dose changes made today. 

History and interval events: 

He attended today for routine review, accompanied by his partner. He has been maintaining regular sleep and avoiding known precipitants such as missed meals and excess caffeine. After experiencing dizziness and headache on two occasions, his symptoms improved without complications. On 15 October his absences settled with treatment. The most recent episode was on 23 October, and since then he has been well. With no recurrence for the past months and overall stable epilepsy, no additional management is indicated at this stage. He has resumed cycling to work and reports no issues with concentration or memory. There is no history of injuries, emergency attendances, or hospital admissions since his last visit. 

Examination: 

General and neurological examination were unremarkable. Vitals stable. No focal deficits observed. 

Plan: 

- Continue Levetiracetam at current dose.  
- Reinforced adherence, sleep hygiene, and avoidance of alcohol excess.  
- He will continue to keep a brief diary of any further episodes and contact us if there is any recurrence or change in pattern.  
- Routine bloods (U+Es, FBC) via GP in 6 months as part of ongoing monitoring. 

Follow-up: 

We will review him in clinic in six months, or sooner if there is any clinical change. 

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Clinic date is 16 January 2022. The letter states: "The most recent episode was on 23 October, and since then he has been well" and "With no recurrence for the past months". There is no evidence of additional seizures between 23 October and 16 January. Thus, there is 1 seizure over approximately 3 months (23 Oct → 16 Jan ≈ 85 days ≈ 2.8 months), normalized to 1 per 3 month. The mentions of dizziness/headache on two occasions are not described as seizures and are not counted.
